name: SLIM
first: First precision landing within 100 m of target on another world
description: Until SLIM, lunar landings had a kilometre-scale targeting accuracy. JAXA's Smart Lander demonstrated 100 m accuracy — the precision required for crewed missions to dock with pre-positioned cargo, for sample-return ascent vehicles to find their orbital partners, and for any future architecture that depends on landing at specific scientifically valuable sites. SLIM also pioneered low-energy ballistic transfer to the Moon, taking 135 days to arrive but using less propellant than direct trajectories. A demonstration of the patience interplanetary missions can afford when timeline isn't the binding constraint.

events.label: BALLISTIC TRANSFER
events.note: Low-energy ballistic capture trajectory in progress.

events.label: TOUCHDOWN
events.note: Soft-landing at Shioli Crater 2024-01-19 15:20 UTC. Within target accuracy: ~55 m off centre.

events.label: TILTED LANDING
events.note: Lander resting nose-down due to engine anomaly during final descent. Solar panels initially in shadow.

events.label: POWER RESUMED
events.note: Sun angle changes; solar panels generate power; operations resumed for one lunar day.
